title: Albertson-Berman Conjecture
statement_text: Every planar graph has an induced forest on at least half of its vertices, i.e., $a(G) \geq |V(G)|/2$ for every planar graph $G$.
context_text: Conjectured in 1979 by Albertson and Berman; the best known result toward it is Borodin's Acyclic 5-Color Theorem, which implies $a(G) \geq 2|V(G)|/5$. If true, it would be tight for $K_4$.
paper_contribution: Introduces Conjecture 1.1 as a fractional generalization and notes that Borodin's Acyclic 5-Color Theorem implies every planar graph has fractional vertex-arboricity at most $5/2$.

title: Akiyama-Watanabe Conjecture
statement_text: Every bipartite planar graph has an induced forest on at least five-eighths of its vertices, i.e., $a(G) \geq 5|V(G)|/8$ for every bipartite planar graph $G$.
context_text: Conjectured in 1987 by Akiyama and Watanabe; the best known result is $a(G) \geq (4|V(G)|+3)/7$ (Wang, Xie, and Yu). The conjecture may hold more generally for triangle-free planar graphs, for which Le proved $a(G) \geq 5|V(G)|/9$. If true, it would be tight for the cube.
paper_contribution: Introduces Conjecture 1.2 as a fractional generalization, strengthened to all triangle-free planar graphs.

title: Kowalik-Lužar-Škrekovski Conjecture
statement_text: Every planar graph of girth at least five has an induced forest on at least seven-tenths of its vertices, i.e., $a(G) \geq 7|V(G)|/10$ for every planar graph $G$ of girth at least five.
context_text: Conjectured in 2010; the best known result prior to this paper is $a(G) \geq 2n/3$ proved independently by Kelly–Liu and Shi–Xu. If true, it would be tight for the dodecahedron.
paper_contribution: Proves every planar graph of girth at least five has fractional vertex-arboricity at most $2 - 1/324$, which is the first result below 2 and constitutes progress toward the fractional generalization of this conjecture (Conjecture 1.3).

title: Conjecture 1.1
statement_text: Every planar graph has fractional vertex-arboricity at most two.
context_text: This is the fractional generalization of the Albertson-Berman Conjecture: since $|V(G)|/a(G) \leq \mathrm{va}_f(G)$, the conjecture implies $a(G) \geq |V(G)|/2$. If true, it would also imply that every planar graph has fractional chromatic number at most four.

title: Conjecture 1.2
statement_text: Every triangle-free planar graph has fractional vertex-arboricity at most $8/5$.
context_text: This generalizes the Akiyama-Watanabe Conjecture to the fractional setting and simultaneously strengthens it by replacing the hypothesis of bipartite with the weaker assumption of triangle-free.

title: Conjecture 1.3
statement_text: Every planar graph of girth at least five has fractional vertex-arboricity at most $10/7$.
context_text: This is the fractional generalization of the Kowalik-Lužar-Škrekovski Conjecture. The paper's main result (Theorem 1.4) proves the weaker bound of $2 - 1/324$, breaking the integer barrier for the first time.

title: Dvořák-Mnich Conjecture
statement_text: For some $\varepsilon > 0$, planar graphs of girth at least five have fractional chromatic number at most $3 - \varepsilon$.
context_text: The paper notes that since $\chi_f(G) \leq 2 \cdot \mathrm{va}_f(G)$, a bound of $\mathrm{va}_f(G) \leq 3/2 - \varepsilon$ for planar graphs of girth at least five would imply this conjecture; and that Conjecture 1.3, if true, would confirm it in a strong sense.
paper_contribution: Notes that the current bound $\mathrm{va}_f(G) \leq 2 - 1/324$ does not suffice to confirm this conjecture; a bound of $3/2 - \varepsilon$ would be needed.
notes: Year of attribution not stated in the paper text; reference [9] not reproduced in the extracted content.
